column2: Ethernet twisted-pair cable performance is described by a cable {{c1::category}} such as Cat 5e or Cat 6A.
column3: The category describes manufacturing and performance requirements; it is not a brand name.
column4:
Why

The cable category must meet the Ethernet standard being deployed. This is a better A+ recall target than memorizing every possible category table.
column7: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.23; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2

column2: {{c1::T568A and T568B}} are twisted-pair termination standards for 8P8C Ethernet connectors.
column3: Use a consistent termination standard for a normal straight-through cable.
column4:
Exam Trap

The useful Objective 3.2 concept is recognizing the standards and using them consistently. Exact pin-color order is intentionally left out of flashcards here.
column7: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.24; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2
column9: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Cloze T568A T568B Ethernet Source::Messer-v170

column2: UTP means {{c1::unshielded twisted pair}}; STP means {{c2::shielded twisted pair}}.
column3: STP adds shielding for interference protection, while UTP is common for ordinary twisted-pair network cabling.
column4:
Why

This acronym pair is foundational for recognizing copper network cable descriptions in Objective 3.2.
column7: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.23; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2

column2: USB 2.0 and USB 3.0 are generations of the {{c1::Universal Serial Bus}} peripheral standard.
column3: USB is used for common peripherals such as keyboards, mice, printers, and external storage.
column4:
Why

Objective 3.2 names USB 2.0 and USB 3.0 under peripheral cables. The key is recognizing USB as the peripheral connection family.
column7: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.25; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2

column3: HDMI is a digital audio/video connection common on consumer displays, monitors, and projectors.
column4:
Why

The expansion reinforces that HDMI is a multimedia audio/video interface, not a network or storage cable.
column7: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.26; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2
column9: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Cloze HDMI VideoCables Source::Messer-v170

column2: VGA is {{c1::analog}} video, while DVI-D carries {{c2::digital}} video.
column3: DVI variants matter: DVI-A is analog, DVI-D is digital, and DVI-I can include both.
column4:
Exam Trap

The card contrasts VGA with a specific DVI digital variant. Do not assume every DVI connector carries the same signal.
column7: 220-1201 3.2 - Cable Types, Connectors, Features, and Purposes
column8: Professor Messer 220-1201 v1.70 p.26; CompTIA 220-1201 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 3.2
column9: A+::220-1201::3.2 A+::220-1201::Domain3-Hardware A+::220-1201::CableTypesConnectorsFeaturesandPurposes Cloze VGA DVI VideoCables Source::Messer-v170

column2: Common fiber connectors in Objective 3.2 include ST, SC, and {{c1::LC}}.
column3: ST twists to lock, SC pushes on, and LC is a smaller connector with a clip.
column4:
Why

The objective names ST, SC, and LC connector types. Recognizing their lock styles is useful, but the exact product shape can vary by manufacturer.
